mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which underpins corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS MoM growth is followed by negative returns in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 9.0%, it signals sustained labor market improvement, boosting consumer confidence and spending, thereby driving equity markets higher over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 60% of occurrences, or the mean return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) is rising month-over-month while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1.0%, the economy faces negative real interest rates and inflationary pressure, which erodes real earnings and prompts future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the Rank IC over all triggered instances is below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession expansion regime defined by two consecutive quarters of positive GDP growth, rising industrial production (INDPRO) confirms broad recovery and accelerates earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth positive for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: In periods when GDPC1 growth is positive for two quarters, the correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.10, or the direction hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) rise above 600 thousand units at a seasonally adjusted annual rate, it signals a recovery in the housing sector, which supports construction employment and consumer wealth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 600, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate synchronized labor and manufacturing strength, which reinforces corporate profitability and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM increase > 0 and INDPRO MoM increase > 0
failure_condition: When both triggers are satisfied, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate of at least 25 basis points within a month signals an unexpected tightening of monetary policy, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by >= 0.25 percentage points within a month
failure_condition: When the trigger occurs, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average excess return over risk-free rate is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) create a powerful positive sentiment shock, indicating broad demand strength, which propels short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM increase > 0 and RSAFS MoM increase > 0
failure_condition: When both triggers are met, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of occurrences, or the Rank IC over all joint events is below 0.05.
